design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore dungeon rooms, destroy enemy waves with projectile weapons, collect keys to unlock doors, manage depleting health bar by collecting food items, progress through multiple exits to advance levels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Constant health depletion over time creates urgency; enemy mass and dungeon complexity require tactical positioning and resource prioritization
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Four-player cooperative dungeon crawling with arcade-style action and fantasy combat, evolving into RPG-style character progression in Quest mode
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Gauntlet IV bridges arcade tradition with home console features: maintains the relentless, punishing arcade design (constant health drain) while adding home-console amenities (save system, RPG leveling, character customization). Quest mode philosophy treats progression as earned through combat mastery plus stat investment rather than pure skill alone

difficulty.checkpointing: Quest mode allows save game progress; Arcade mode uses multiple exits per stage offering branching paths; Record mode tracks progress via password
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character leveling and item purchasing in Quest mode provide power scaling to offset arcade difficulty; four-player cooperation reduces per-player load; multiple stage exits allow skill-based routing decisions

progression.upgrade_system: RPG-style character leveling system in Quest mode; item purchasing mechanics allowing attribute enhancement; character classes with distinct progression paths
progression.unlock_mechanism: Keys collected in dungeons unlock doors; progression through sequential dungeon stages; multiple stage exits unlock branching level paths
replayability_hooks: Four distinct game modes (Arcade, Quest, Battle, Record) with different objectives
Multiplayer cooperation enabling different strategies per playthrough
Multiple exits per stage creating branching dungeon layouts
Character leveling and item customization in Quest mode
Deathmatch Battle mode for PvP competition
Password system in Record mode for challenge runs

world_building_techniques: Progressive stage design suggesting deeper descent into underworld
Enemy variety and placement implying inhabited fantasy realm

boss_design.philosophy: Quest mode features new bosses designed specifically for Genesis version, suggesting boss encounters serve as major progression gates and challenge moments distinct from wave-based room combat

danger_signals: Constant health depletion mechanic creates time-pressure stress
Enemy wave quantity creates overwhelming visual/mechanical load
Multiple simultaneous player damage in cooperative mode can create cooperation friction
environmental_storytelling: Dungeon architecture progression suggests descent narrative
Locked doors and key collection establish puzzle-solving world logic
Enemy placement density indicates inhabited hostile realm
